name: Suspicious Activity Followed by Virtualization Administration Task
notes: This rule triggers when suspicious activities (e.g. multiple virtual machines are deleted, credential exfiltration detected, etc) are discovered followed by regular administration tasks in a virtualized environment (e.g. creation or deletion of a virtual machine, roles update, etc).
bb_references: BB:BehaviorDefinition: Regular Virtualization Administration match at least 1 times in 10 minutes after any of BB:BehaviorDefinition: Suspicious Virtualization Activities match with the same Username
conditions_text: when BB:BehaviorDefinition: Regular Virtualization Administration match at least 1 times in 10 minutes after any of BB:BehaviorDefinition: Suspicious Virtualization Activities match with the same Username
thresholds: when BB:BehaviorDefinition: Regular Virtualization Administration match at least 1 times in 10 minutes after any of BB:BehaviorDefinition: Suspicious Virtualization Activities match with the same Username
TD_list: APPLY Suspicious Activity Followed by Virtualization Administration Task on events which are detected by the LOCAL system

AND when BB:BehaviorDefinition: Regular Virtualization Administration match at least 1 times in 10 minutes after any of BB:BehaviorDefinition: Suspicious Virtualization Activities match with the same Username
RED_list: Event name: Suspicious Activity Followed by Virtualization Administration Task
Event description: Detects normal administration tasks in virtualized environment performed after a suspicious activity has been detected with the same username.
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Username
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
